reviews.review_text: Camped
Highly recommend staying here, great customer service, great location and great cabins. We'll definitely be back. Thank you Big 4 Rainbow Beach. 😍👌👍🏼💜 From the Thaiday's.

Big 4 can't control the weather but they sure can control the comfort or not if that is what you prefer.  If you choose a non powered site renovated bathroom, showers and kitchen with kettles, toasters, fridges, and even microwaves. They've got amazing campgrounds right off the waters edge and an entire fridge of beer at standard prices. Believe this a 6 pack for $20. Hot damn. Clean and maintained facilities, grounds and also one of those colourful big balloon bouncing dome. Everything you absolutely need for the very best week of camping of your life for whatever your family and friends needs.

Je suis resté 4 nuits dans ce camping début octobre 2025, emplacement voiture roof-tent. Le camping est propres, tout les endroits communs sont bien tenue et agréable. Le camping est bien situé, proche du port et aussi de la ville. Seul bémol dès le début de soirée on se fait piquer le partout par des moustiques et des petits moucherons. Je sais que ce n'est pas la faute du camping mais ça rend l'expérience un peu désagréable par moment.

What an experience at the Rainbow Beach Big 4 Holiday Park. We were a little nervous booking after reviews said there were Midgees around so we armed ourselves with every insecticide available on the market including the old fashioned mosquito coils and off we went.
From the time of arrival we knew it was going to be a great place for a few days. The park entrance was very well manicured, neat and tidy. Reception was warm and welcoming so by the time we drove through the boom gate and down to our site past all the cabins with beautiful gardens we were feeling pretty excited. Our site was amazing with easy access and healthy green grassy sites we were set for a great time. The ablution blocks were cleaned daily (doesn’t happen in every park I can tell you) and very tidy indeed. All the Staff were so friendly and nothing was a problem for anyone. Stayed 4 nights and NO Midgees so happy days. The sunsets over Tin Can Bay strait are gorgeous and you can even have a campfire to add to the ambience of the holiday.
Great spot, fantastic Staff, beautiful well presented park with a Thai food van on site to save you cooking on Friday nights. Loved our stay and never had to break out the insecticide for the Midgees.

Had to cancel our one night stay unfortunately, tried to ask for a refund, was happy to pay a fee. But they are pretty happy to take your full $245.
Off peak time, I don’t really understand that they wouldn’t even offer 50% back. I had told them we planned to return later in the coming months. Not even a swap of dates.
Young parents with a one year old. That money is pretty devastating to lose in today’s cost of living. Given that big 4 parks have a family vibe/ reputation you’d think they’d show some empathy.

This is my second ever bad review as I only usually give out positive ones . Disappointing, when we eventually visit we will be staying elsewhere. Not at this greedy venue

Great spot, we did camp on a powered site. It is really beautiful,  only disappointment was we didn't book longer.
Do pack repellent as you do get bitten lol joys of being so close to the water and living in beautiful Qld.

Had a great stay. No issues at all checking staff were great |Cabin was clean and suited the 2 off us for our one night stay |Was a bit apprehensive due to some of the poor reviews but they seem to be from people struggling with a “you get what you pay for” mentality |Have no issues recommending this place for a getaway |Out of town but that’s what we signed up for |Head down to the boat ramp and watch the sunset is a must take a few drinks with you 🍻
Great location on the water with plenty of kids play things.  20 minute walk to the town.  Clezn facilities and well maintained.  Just bring the midgee spray.
A great spot, away from the busyness but close enough to the town and beach. Great open spaces for all to enjoy.

Definitely won't be rushing back any time soon! We stayed here 6 months ago and loved it, but were incredibly disappointed this time by the filthy amenities, unkept grounds, draconian rules by new management and sky high prices.
Photos posted are of the amenity block in the main unpowered section of the park, and were taken immediately after the cleaner finished cleaning.
I'm convinced that management just don't care, as I complained twice and also sent the photos.

Would not stay here again. The amenities are disgusting. They need to either hire a cleaner or fire the current one. Price for an unpowered site is expensive for the facilities offered. The midgies were unbearable and noise from the camp next to me until 230am (I had to ask them to quieten down) was enough for me to write this review.
Clean and nice

Nice facilities and friendly staff. Not overly packed. We ended up leaving a night early due to the midges/sandflies being unbearable, even will good repellent. The playground was good for toddlers. The games room could definitely do with an update, lots of machines not working and a bit run down. Close to Rainbow Beach which was great.

Campsite is close to the water. The place are ok and the campsite is big. The shower and toilets are good. However the kitchen not super clean, and the campsite management is not really flexible despite he was almost empty, be aware to not make any mistake in your booking otherwise money is money, they won't be nice.

We stayed 2 nights (out of our booked 3),  on an unpowered camping site we chose close-ish to the water.

Bins, showers, toilets, communal kitchen all within a stones throw from unpowered camping areas.
No complaints on the showers and toilets. It's a caravan park, not the Hilton. Not super clean but good enough for a wash.

The grounds were nice, and there's a nice view if setup in a good spot.

What really lets this location down is the midgees. No fault of the owners, but being close to the water - there's inevitably insects. This place is no exception and you will get eaten alive, even with stupid amounts of deet.

We enjoyed our 2 nights, but after some exploring on day 3 and arriving back to rain and someone having setup 10 metres away from us when the entire park was basically empty, we weren't prepared to deal with the midges for another night.

Thanks for having us. We will definitely be returning to Rainbow Beach.

Pasamos una noche en el camping y fue espectacular. Las instalaciones geniales, la cocina también y la tranquilidad que nos dio nos encanto. Todo genial, lo único malo es que hay muchos mosquitos 🦟 El atardecer en el muelle un 10!

Stayed in a cabin and it was better than I expected, clean and solid with great shower and excellent water pressure. It actually felt like a self catering hotel room not a 'Donga' like most parks.
Too busy working to see the sights, it is about 4kms from the town too.
I was warned of the vicious midges out at the site by a local, but did not get attacked, mind you I was not camping and stayed inside. Best be prepared tho'
Fantastic  place, good value. Will return.

I will never ever stay here again. We had to cancel our holiday 1 month before our holiday to the big 4 caravan park due to the rise of fuel prices. I got no refund as I was told our booking was in a busy period (we booked outside school holidays).
I will be telling others of the poor management of this situation. People are doing it tough and this is how we are treated.
No beach. Not a good place during midgie season. Otherwise a nice park
Not worth the money
Best camping I know. Acommodations are very clean and pretty new, all of them very comfotable. Lot of things to do there. Highly recommended
沒有設施，外圍也沒有景點，說明是有釣魚的，但碼頭標示不可釣魚，工作人員服務也一般

Honestly wouldn't stay here if they paid me to come poor guy paid to stay here but his wife she got sick and passed away and they still won't refund the money he spent to stay at this park. Where is your compassion if someone spent money with your company and has a real traumatic reason they can't stay they should be entitled to a refund not messed around and told to contact the big office when the big office has said you need to contact Rainbow big 4. Does anyone seeing these reviews think this is unfair also check out sunny coast community board full story to read is there

Im contacting you about the Big 4 caravan park Rainbow Beach. |The manager called me today 16/02/2026 to tell me the owners refused to give me a refund the reason is people get sick and have to cancel their holiday. |This is why my wife had to cancel the holiday. |Due to my wife being diagnosed with undifferentiated pancreatic cancer she wanted to go on holiday from Thursday 9th January 2025 to Sunday 12th January 2025 at the big 4 holiday park rainbow beach. Unfortunately we had to cancel due to starting chemotherapy. The big 4 put the booking into credit that we had 1 year to use. My wife got too weak to climb in and out of a roof top tent. Unfortunately 9 months later my wife passed away from pancreatic cancer. I phoned up the big 4 caravan park they said i can still use the credit along i book before January 2026. But on the the 16/12/2025 I phoned the big 4 and asked them for a refund they said send your bank details and they will look at it. I phoned back to find out what is happening about the refund they said they sent it to head office for them to approve the refund. I phoned the head office and they told me it's up to the big 4 rainbow beach caravan park to give me a refund because each park are operated independently. I phoned the big 4 rainbow beach again they said it's up to head office to give a refund.  I phoned head office again they said it's up to them to refund and  they will phone rainbow beach to discuss

We had such an incredible stay at BIG4 Breeze Holiday Parks – Rainbow Beach.

The camping spots are huge and being right on the beach is something special. Waking up to the ocean, stepping straight onto the sand and having that space around you makes it feel like a true coastal escape.

The location is perfect, so close to Rainbow Beach and everything you need, yet still peaceful and relaxed. It is the best of both worlds.

The facilities are excellent. Clean, well maintained and thoughtfully set up for campers. Everything feels organised and easy, which makes the whole experience even better.

Such a great spot for a beachside getaway. We will definitely be camping here again.
A nice self contained cabin that suited our overnight stay. We enjoyed the spa bath.

Left early mosquito so bad, we also had a disability cabin. As will as a caravan site.
Disability shower is dangerous the tiles are so slippery, even after drying the with towels, was no mop, had to ask for one. Our family had a cabin at the same time, it was disgusting, when the complaint was given a smaller cabin, which was just as bad and no refund for a smaller cabin. Wouldn't go back
Lovely staff, good location & dog friendly.  Dog friendly cabin was clean & quiet. Beds were very comfortable, good pillows & the towels were better than some 5 star hotels.

Nous avons réservé un peux au jour le jour et la réception (téléphone et mail) nous à répondu rapidement et facilement ! Possibilité d'arriver après l'heure de fermeture de la réception un gros plus pour ceux qui ne savent pas trop l'heure d'arrivée ou qui arrive tard !
Merci beaucoup !
Les douches, sanitaire et cuisine son propre et il y a de la place 👍👌
Bonne adresse pour le camping 🏕️

Beautiful place,  clean areas, near main attractions,  the staff is friendly and accommodating.

Our cabin was comfortable,  clean with air-conditioning

It has a huge area for  unpowered sites, allowed a fire , helped keep the midges away but great spot ,
Had takeaway trucks there both nights one night Thai food and the second was burgers or baked spuds

I was part of a group staying at the powered sites ( booked in by my step daughter) and she was told one car per site. I asked if I could drive in and unload abd drive back out to repark elsewhere. Was told no and it was a $15 fee to do so..I am in remission for cancer removed from my leg so I had quite an ordeal walking 15 mins to and from my car in 30 degree heat nearly fainting and breaking down in pain from doing so! I am sorry but this fee is a joke! And as for walking distance to nearby shops ahd cafes is far from the truth! It's a drive away! The middies were horrible and I ended up heading back home the day after I arrived to head 600ks home. Will not recommend this place ever! Will never stay there ever. The $15 fee is highway robbery!
Very nice campground

We stayed one night with our one-year-old. While the location is convenient, the cleanliness of the cabin was disappointing. The floor was not properly cleaned, which made me hesitant to let my baby sit or play on the floor. We also noticed there were no extra bin bags or spare toilet paper provided, which was inconvenient, especially when travelling with a young child. Cleanliness and basic supplies could definitely be improved

We booked two nights while passing through but checked out after one night.||The staff at reception were genuinely lovely, and unfortunately that was the highlight of our stay. We checked into a “villa” advertised as sleeping six, but it felt cramped and overcrowded. The toilet was broken on arrival and required maintenance to fix it.||On a very hot day, the air-conditioning struggled and the cabin became uncomfortably warm. We were travelling with four children, and two sets of bunk beds had been squeezed into a tiny room, leaving no space for them to store their bags, which had to remain in the living area. Our room was also extremely tight, fitting only an old-style double bed and a single bedside table.||The villa did not feel particularly clean — sand tracked easily throughout the space and there was no vacuum provided to help manage this in such a small cabin.||For a Big4 park, the amenities felt run down, there were no organised activities for kids, and the Wi-Fi was very slow.||Overall, it did not meet expectations for a family-friendly stay, which is why we chose to leave early.

I rarely leave reviews be it good or bad but this place infuriated me. Have stayed at a fair few BIG 4s but this place, by far the worst and surprised they are even associated. Arrived just before reception closed. Air con in room 48 (2 bedroom) was set at 24 upon arrival on a humid day as you do knowing guests are arriving, hot box basically, there's only air con in lounge area, not bedrooms.Turned down the mouldy, smelly aircon to 20 whilst we left to go to dinner, arrived back 1.5 hours later, no difference, hot as heck. Dragged the 3 mattresses to the lounge area around 9.45pm as it felt a bit cooler I mean a 3 degree drop maybe, kid you not, because my 3 children couldn't sleep after multiple cold showers, window so tiny in their room so no air flow, screen frames bent in main room, tears aswell in both bedroom windows so it created a gap......we all got eaten alive with 2 of us being allergic to whatever bit us as we have red lumpy blisters and this was definitely from inside the cabin because I never went outside the cabins for long or had time to explore rainbow beach, just dinner inside a pub. Tv internet don't even bother, even normal tv a hassle. Bathroom tiny. Games room appaling, dirty when I ducked my head in at checkin. Advertised to comfortabley sleep 6 and air con. Would never return nor recommend even in an "upgraded" room.
Stayed here to go on the K,gari two day bus tour, I was welcomed with a lovely smile by the team members. The accomdatation was so lovely and clean.

Disappointing stay – left early due to bites and poor response

I booked 3 nights at Big 4 Rainbow Beach but left after 2 nights because I did not want to suffer another night of bites.

Although the bed looked clean, my friends, I and my family were bitten repeatedly during the night and woke up with many red, itchy bites on our legs (photos attached). This was very uncomfortable and concerning.

My friends went to the reception to request a room change, but the request was rejected, and no compensation was offered. Because of this response, I did not make a further complaint at the counter and chose to leave early instead.

Cleanliness and guest safety are basic expectations, and the way this issue was handled was disappointing. I hope management takes this seriously and improves room inspections and customer service to prevent this from happening again.
Millions of biting midges! Make sure you have insect repellent with you.

Stayed here for 5 days had a 3 bedroom cabin. Only 1 of the bedrooms had aircon. 3 kids had a time room with 4 bunks and no fan or aircon ,Kids knocking on cabin after 9pm abusing our kids, stovetop didn’t work, aircon was colder than the fridge was. Cutlery and crockery was filthy and disgusting. Smart TV connected to park wifi  but couldn’t get any internet, tried to connect it to my phone but then defaulted back to the non existent park wifi. Tv reception was so pixelated it was unwatchable.
$2600 for 5 days absolute NOT worth it , very disappointed and won’t be back here.

DO NOT STAY HERE. By far the most horrendous stay we had on our trip down the east coast. Everything in the kitchen was filthy and greasy, including the bbq’s outside. Not enough bathroom facilities for the amount of people staying there. I went into the bathroom in the morning and someone had spewed all over the toilet and on each side next to it and hasn’t even attempted to clean it. There were definitely some young people partying there and I think there should be a strict no alcohol policy to avoid something like this. And I assume this is not the first time this holiday park is receiving this volume of people so they should better cater for it. Have all facilities cleaned AT LEAST two times a day. Especially when paying $54 for an unpowered site.

⚠️ 1 STAR – WARNING TO FAMILIES ⚠️|My family and I have stayed at many BIG4 parks across Queensland over the years and have always had great experiences. This was the only park we’ve ever had an issue with.|We stayed at BIG4 Rainbow Beach Holiday Park for two nights, paying over $700. The experience did not come close to justifying the price.|The manager, Merle, was rude, aggressive, and dismissive. When concerns were raised, I was brushed off entirely.|Our cabin had multiple maintenance issues that were reported repeatedly with no resolution, apology, room change, or proper compensation.|Most concerning, the manager and maintenance spent 45 minutes inside our family’s cabin during our stay, which felt intrusive and uncomfortable, yet the manager saw no issue with this.|A 15% discount was offered and declined, as this is no better than standard booking discounts on other booking platforms. Responsibility was then deflected by accusing my partner of speeding through the park, despite him driving 10km/h in line with the posted signage, with the manager claiming the limit was 5km/h.|This park is extremely poorly operated. Based on our experience, I would not recommend this park to families. Unprofessional, uncomfortable, and disappointing.

My family and I have stayed at many BIG4 parks across Queensland over the years and have always had great experiences. This was the only park we’ve ever had an issue with.

We stayed at BIG4 Rainbow Beach Holiday Park for two nights, paying over $700. The experience did not come close to justifying the price.

The manager, Merle, was rude, aggressive, and dismissive. When concerns were raised, I was brushed off entirely.
Our cabin had multiple maintenance issues that were reported repeatedly with no resolution, with the first maintenance issue being at 3am in the morning and continued throughout our stay.
No offer of room change, or proper compensation. Just a careless minimising apology.

Most concerning, the manager and maintenance spent 45 minutes inside our family’s cabin during our stay, which felt intrusive and uncomfortable, yet the manager saw no issue with this.

After expressing to the manager how unacceptable this is I was eventually offered a 15% discount which I declined as this is no better than standard booking discounts on other online booking platforms and does not come close to the reflection of the poor experience we had.

Responsibility was then deflected by accusing my partner of speeding through the park, despite him driving 10km/h in line with the posted signage, with the manager claiming the limit was 5km/h.
This park is extremely poorly operated.
Based on our experience, I would not recommend this park to families. Unprofessional, uncomfortable, and disappointing.
I would give no stars if that was an option.
Hoy 24-12-25 me isieron un robo en mi cuenta. Me sacaron dinero sin mi autorización. Quiero que me la regresen a mi cuenta

No swimming, eaten alive morning and evening. Most shower heads could be maintained far better as most showers either have 5-10% pressure avail or seals missing and water sprays to ceiling to the floor.
Great beach holiday with everything you need in one place highly recommended
Super emplacement

We had the 2 bedroom cabin and it was very clean, great aircon and everything was there that we needed.

The holiday park itself was  clean and had some activities for the kids.

It would have been 100% if there was a pool for the kids and wifi.

Overall we had a good experience!

Thank you

Stayed on a powered sites. Even though the park was very quiet, we were allocated one right next to a loud bunch in the sun all day. Even though they have shady sites around. Midgies galore. Really not close to town, beach etc. I wouldn't say it's value for money.

We stayed here a couple of nights before our K’gari tour. Got a 2 bed cabin and it was perfect. Nice place, right by the water, great resort. Had a little shop, cafe and good laundry facilities. The staff were so helpful too. The maintenance guys were so kind.

We also had our taxi cancel on us on the morning we were due to leave for our tour (only 1 taxi in town and we were really stuck). But Maureen who worked there was so kind and drove us into town so we didn’t miss our tour, thank you so much! Can’t recommend this place enough.

Beware of this caravan park Big 4 paid a deposit circumstances changed cancelled booking within 20 hrs and they charged a $39 cancellation fee and refunded $17. Also did same to other party with us. Been a big 4 member for years never experienced this. Money grabbing grubs. Wouldn’t recommend them. The female staff member was extremely blunt with my friend when he questioned it. Don’t stay there.

Un superbe camping situé directement en bord de mer. Attention, les routes d'accès et les emplacements de camping sont fait de sable. Nous avons tout de même pu nous y garer avec un véhicule 2 roues motrices sans soucis.
Et un 2e attention, des midges sont présentes sur le camping, ce qui fait qu'il faut une moustiquaire si vous voulez vous installer en extérieur. Sinon les midges vous piqueront en toute discrétion, et vous sentirez les petites piqûres rouges rondes qui démangent après coup. Les mouches font à peine 1mm ce qui les rend difficile à voir, et elles sont présentes par milliers
Very clean nice comfortable astmosphere made us feel very welcome.

Check in war super und sehr freundlich.Wir konnten Online vorher eine Site mit Strom buchen.Leider gab es ein Gewitter und der Strom fiel aus und es war ziemlich matschig.Bis morgens gab es keinen Strom und kein warmes Wasser.Ein schönes Erlebnis war am Nachmittag die ganzen Krabben bei Ebbe zu sehen.Perfekt war auch, dass wir dort abgeholt wurden für unsere Tour nach K’Gari.
Hat sich auf jeden Fall gelohnt.
Easy stay, liked it here a lot, good not so great but everything else was great

Don’t do it, we got a cabin to find mould everywhere, beds were very small and uncomfortable, dishes were dirty, ceiling fans were filthy, shower was fit for someone no taller then 5 foot.
Very disappointed

Booked a grass site for one night thought we might stay a few nights but it was nothing but sand and when it rained it became disgusting. Midgies and mosquitoes were so thick we couldn’t wait to leave the next morning. Internet wasn’t working so I couldn’t work. Would have been nice to be told that when booking in. Worst caravan park we stayed at in 10 weeks and the most expensive.
Well kept grounds. Great amenities. Close to the water but not too crowded.
Very short drive into the town. Bathrooms were clean. There is not much to do in the area, we only stayed here for a night either side of our Fraser Island trip, so it was perfect for us.

Stayed at powered site behind cabins bins over flowing smelt 😡 flies everywhere bugs 🐜 also
Reported to office to say we are leaving 3 days early 🤔response was we have contacted about the bins havent had a response 🤔well when at home 🏠 if you call about bins not being emptied they email you back
Said they would send maintenance 🧑‍🔧 to look 👀 no show
No ice as freezer broken😡 showers need to be upgraded
Have stayed at other big 4 caravan parks and loved them
No offer of refund🥵

Average cabin. We got the superior 2 bedroom and it only had 1 aircon which wasn’t great. The floors were dirty as which wasn’t great when my 13 month old is crawling still. Beds are hard as rocks too, wasn’t comfy. Staff were great though and location was good. Jumping pillow looked to be broken or permantly not working anymore. Which was a bummer with for our 2.5 year old. Would be great with a pool also but again they didn’t have one. I wouldn’t stay again for the money I spent.

By far the worst holiday park we’ve come to during our 7 week road trip. It is way overcrowded, the sites are dirty and poorly separated (our power cord needs to go through another campsite to reach us), there is zero wifi connectivity despite it being advertised, it is nowhere near anything of interest in Rainbow Beach.

Got the spa cabin and the spa didnt work. The worst shower I've ever had, hot water all the way on and then as soon as you try put cold with it goes too cold, no matter what you did couldn't get a warm shower. Only 1 aircond in the whole unit, not good enough to reach the back room and was hot in there. All in all cabin is very old and requiring maintenance that has been lacking. They dont have a pool either. Def way better option in town especially for the money they charge. I would have thought they would have offered a discount on the stay when I told them about the issues but nope didn't look like it was ever a thought. The facility is extremely clean and tidy and the staff were friendly enough, but after being out fishing all day all you want is a nice warm shower and that was just impossible unfortunately. I.would recommend going  for an alternative accommodation option as there are quite a few others.
What a perfect overnight stop before hitting the early morning barge over to fraser-Kgari. The park offers lots of accommodation options. Great convenient location. Thank you

Great park, spacious sites. A bit sandy at the back. Nice and quiet. Location depends on what you like to do and if you are immune to midgies it’s nice. We had leave because of the midgies. 5 minute drive to town

We enjoyed our stay in august ,clean amenities, generous sized camp sites ,friendly staff ✅️ handy to the boat ramp,no bug issues but it is winter atm, we have returned for another stay on a powered site 👍🌈🌞

So honestly, we stayed in a budget cabin. #45 to be exact. We weren't expecting glamor, we paid 158aud. What we WERE expecting was a functional shower, it even boldly advertises "rainfall shower head" on expedia.. We didnt get that. The shower is made for someone who's 5 feet or shorter. It literally comes up to my husband's chest, hes 6'4. Im 5'7 and had to crouch to wash my hair. Photo included. This is kind of an idiotic design flaw for a cabin with high ceilings, capable of having a normal height shower head. I realize to meet NCC's code you need to have a certain amount of tile above the shower head, but it was possible to meet code, tile higher and not have a 5 foot shower head in this cabin. Pretty disappointing to spend almost 200 a night to struggle in the shower to even wash our hair after a beach day. Besides that, cabin is dated but functional, amenities are cool and park is very family friendly. Would stay again, but would request a normal sized shower capable of properly showering in.
Slecht

The location for fishing is spot on. |The ladies in reception warm and friendly.|A large park, catering to all sorts of travellers.|Would recommend they get better cleaners for the amenities. ( as I work in the industry, the amenities were only 60%)|Pet friendly which is great

We stayed in a cabin and it was spotless, comfortable, and well-equipped no complaints there.

However, the family facilities were underwhelming:
No swimming pool and no safe beach area to swim, despite advertising kayak/SUP hire.
Slushie machine out of order and the drinks fridge was almost empty.
Jumping pillow deflated/out of order, which was a let-down for the kids.
Lots of kids riding bikes and swearing at each other around the park.
Staff were friendly, but these issues didn’t seem to be a priority to fix.

Overall, great accommodation but poor on activities and maintenance not what we expected for a family stay.

Update: The photo for the SUP as advertised is not at this location.
We left our caravan stored here for 4 nights, while we went to Fraser Island. Stayed the night we got back. Very good showers, toilets and camp kitchen .
Loved the location and the vast open spaces to set up your campsite. Beautiful spot for your family and friends to have a holiday

Plus d’un cinquantaine de piqûres de « midgies » et moustiques en seulement 1 soirée.
Cela signifie que de 16h00 jusqu’au lendemain matin 9h00, vous ne pouvez pas rester à l’extérieur sans souffrir de dizaines de piqûres.
Le camping est pourtant agréable, avec beaucoup de place, mais il est impossible d’en profiter pleinement.

Nous avons dû louer un Airbnb pour le reste du séjour, tant il était impossible de rester ici.
Great camp grounds. Great toilet/shower facilities. Camp kitchen is a massive plus. But one 2 sinks 2 stoves for the whole camp site. There is a line for washing up dishes. Bring your phones.

Worst experience we book 6mnths in advance with group of friends there is no pool. They put us on sand location even though I asked for grass area or slab concrete. We were double booked and had to park somewhere else as they gave our spot to turn up people I have two very young children. We were bitten constantly to the point I had allergic reaction with welts all over my body. My children were constantly dirty even after shower cause of the sand dirt site attracted more sandflies. You never felt clean trapping sand ever where from the sand site. Power keep tripping out of our caravan we had to move our caravan twice from being double booked causing damage to our pop top.

School holidays! Probably should have known better …
Unsupervised young children everywhere screaming, riding bikes, playing footy etc till 11pm, causing havoc on their bikes for anyone driving in and out of the park, parental language  disgusting, drone came into our annex ( what happened to privacy), shower/toilet blocks disgustingly filthy all day.

Super Campingplatz riesige Stellplätze umd äußerst gepflegte saubere große Sanitärbereiche in ausreichender Anzahl. Die Grillplätzte und Gemeinschaftsküchen sind ebenfalls sehr großzügig. Guter Zugang zum Meer mit Bootsrampe. alles Top👍
Brilliant place,the staff are amazing, so friendly and helpful. Love the reception girls and the maintenance team,unreal.Great cabins, just a beautiful park.
Other than no TV reception ,all else was very good
Amazing place
Excellent  caravan park ,very clean and accommodating
Great unpowered area
Great place to say.  Friendly staff.  Clean amenities block & camp kitchen.  I will be back soon.

The caravan park is huge & it's lovely to be camped spaciously  amongst the trees. You can have a fire in a fire pit, which is always a winner in the evening.  I think you need to like chilling, fishing or 4WDing if you stay a while. There are not many roads, other than the road out.
Great for a week's stay!

Great location for families, not far from the town.
Good playground, great fire pit and kitchens.

We booked to stay in a powered site for 4 nights but loved it and booked a further 3, after coming from a park where we were squashed in like sardines, the big4 sites were huge and an absolute dream, fantastic amenities, park is very clean, a short drive to town and the courtesy bus from the sports club will come and pick you up, we will definitely be back to beautiful Rainbow Beach and Big4

Nice location and very fairly priced. Cabin was okay, however, the ambient noise from other cabins is so loud, could very clearly hear people walking (which sounded like stomping) around their cabins. I would stay again for sure.

Great open park for caravans / motor homes and a good number of cabins. Amenities were clean and tidy, park grounds looked well maintained. My only complaint and in my  opinion a very dangerous accident waiting to happen...... the entry access to the park from CARLO road is on a 90° blind corner at the end of a long straight road and if the parking area is full from other visitors vehicles booking in you are left waiting on CARLO road waiting to book in. A terrible accident waiting to happen.

Clean the toilet shower area very well attended.check in was good.it is on a waterway but 3 kls out of town .it's dog friendly.people coming and going on a daily basis.were in a caravan.pub and sports club courtesy bus.we did sports club only to happy to help for pick up drop off.we had a lovely time there.2clubs one pub  to choose from.staff at big 4 more than helpful and the sports club delightful staff.we had nice food hamburger with chips weS good.main road to town needs bike paths that would make it.maybe in time. A d see  delightful stay.lots of cabins I counted at least 45.people fishing on little platoon.i don't think they got fish.peliacans at sundown.loveley.cover up for middies we haven't been bitten.common sense people.we can say it is crying out for a pool. And the concrete  paths to town..but you can bike it in.
Great place to stay. Clean amenities, great staff

Nothing grassy about powered sites numbers C1 & C2. We camped on a big pile of dirty sand and with a dog and toddler it made for a very unrelaxing getaway. Note to self: double check what condition the ground surface is like if you are particular about cleanliness inside your living space whether it be a tent or caravan.

We booked a powered grass site and they were wonderful and HUGE! Close to the water yet still relatively close to the amenities which were very well-kept and nice. Lots of hot water, separate toilets AND the option of toilet/shower which is great. Our dog loved it here as well, lots of awesome smells to explore. A barista-made coffee, friendly staff and enough entertainment options for the kids - even on rainy days - made this stay unforgettable.
Great park, staff very friendly,park in very good position close to boat ramp and excellent fishing spots,we thoroughly recommend this park.
Excellent location and very accommodating staff, all the things you need and well maintained camp kitchen showers ect

Way over priced we stayed 3 nights asked on the 1st day if we could stay just 2 nights said yes but no refund wi fi didn't work security gate didn't work cabin small and old paint peeling of the walls had to pay $50 extra for our dog for cleaning they said but our cabin stunk as soon as we entered so the $50 cleaning bill is a rip off definitely would not recommend and would never go back there we are now at Harvey Bay and same price no $50 fee for the dog and a hell of a lot nicer cabin

We loved our stay over the past week at Breeze Rainbow Beach. My relatives were in the best 2 room cabin with amazing facilities. I was on a powered site, with access to hotel like ensuites at the amentities block and the best caravan park camp kitchen... We are booking for next June!

Schöner Campingplatz. Zum Rainbow Beach sind es ein paar Kilometer (nicht Fußläufig). Wir waren mit unseren Motor Home dort für eine Nacht. Unser Stellplatz war groß und direkt bei den Toiletten und Duschen, die wirklich sehr schön und modern waren.
Für Kinder gibt es einen Spielplatz und eine Hüpffläche.

We stayed here 3 nights with our Pug, our Cabin was fully self-contained so we were able to cook meals, very clean and comfortable. The cabin included a sofa, full size fridge/freezer, combined mini hotplate oven, and everything we needed for our stay. The grounds are very well kept. A short walk through the back of the park is Carlo Point Marina.
Good value, very comfortable and a shop on site that has a heap of essentials including alcohol
Big grass sites for larger caravan setups. Plenty of room on the beach sites too.

Nous sommes arrivés sous un déluge de pluie et en dehors des heures de réception mais le propriétaire est venu pour nous donner la clef.|Le bungalow était nickel, la literie très confortable et nous avons très bien dormi malgré le déluge qui a continué toute la nuit.

Great location but we got eaten by sandflies even with personal spray on us. On site G we shared a power pole and to access it we had to run a lead over the next site so traffic on that site would have to drive over our lead. Not the best.  Close to everything but the sandflies made it a no for us.
Bit out of the centre of town but a nice quiet spot. Staff friendly and helpful.

Due to unforeseen circumstances with our car, RACQ booked us into the Big 4 Breeze for 4 nights and we had a great time here. The team at Big 4 breeze were so accommodating, the cabins were great and the facilities where perfect for my 4 year old to stay entertained. Definitely need a car as it is a little drive from the main part of rainbow beach. Overall would definitely recommend staying at Big 4 Breeze and we will definitely be back

Facilities are great. Toilet/shower blocks are modern and clean. Staff are friendly and extremely accommodating. Location is great - near rainbow beach town and on the calm water. On the first night I was extremely excited to share this place with my friends, but after days 2,3,4 I unfortunately cannot recommend it due to the midges. I got bitten 200-300 times (I got swarmed when walking back from the toilet block). The itch is excruciating and is lasting several days. My wife's bites have all blistered. We've spent about $200 on preventatives and reparatives. It's so bad we are going to the doctors. It's such a shame, because in no fault of the staff, I cannot give 5 stars. Cheers
Great stay, albeit brief! Had an unpowered camper site and we could choose from a wide area of space to park it in. Good toilets and showers and secure access. Easy check-in outside of hours too.
Best cabin we have ever stayed in
Grumpy lady at reception. Cheer up love
This place offers a great environment at a fair price. However, noise at night was a bit of an issue. Friendly staff and clean facilities make it a good choice.
a bit worn, but clean.
Friendly helpful staff and peaceful, pet friendly location.

Unfortunately our car broke down on the way up to our site.
We reached out hoping that they would be understanding of this ...
Their reply was not
Very disappointed but from the other reviews here it seems like the normal 😕
Great weekend camping trip

Nice little place to stop in for a couple nights.
We wanted to pay to upgrade to a bigger cabin but we were told couldn't as we booked through booking.com...? Although we weren't in the cabin for much of the time, it was average. Bathroom / shower was tiny, but it did have everything we needed for a short stay. We had to drive in town to go to the actual beach. If you wanted to go swimming, you had to walk to the boat ramp / somewhere close to it or through a side access point into the river. Unfortunately no pool either which sucked because it was stinking hot! Our toddler loved the park, it was massive, as well as the jumping pillow. Staff were lovely. Town only a few mins drive away.
What a beautiful place to stay 😍.... the bathrooms were so clean and tidy... plenty of room for the motorhome, and my dogs were welcome.
Stayed in a cabin during school holidays. Overpriced for what you get. Very noisy and busy and certain areas of the camp grounds were quite boggy and muddy. Also got attacked my midgees
Nous avions un Van. Le camping est très beau. Les sanitaires sont propres.
Great place close to everything just take spray for sandflies,you'll have a great time 👍🙏
Great place but will know next time not to tent it in summer as the sandflies where prolific. Cabin stay for me 😄

We stayed here 11-18 January and had the best time. The cabin was very clean and had everything we needed. The park grounds were very well maintained and it was great to see the staff taking care of it daily. We had minor issue with the hot water in the kitchen and it was fixed promptly. The go carts were easy to book and a fun, yet tiring activity. Our girls loved the scavenger hunt and the big pillow. The staff were friendly and helpful. The daily food trucks were a nice touch- pizza, Thai, donuts are a must! Close to the beach and a great base to explore the region.

Great little park, shame it has no pool. The staff were brilliant. Had several messages telling us about the different food vans each night and reminders on noise levels. Showers are a little dated but free and hot! Great location by the boat ramp to watch the sunset and the town is a short drive away.

Customer service is beyond poor, now I understand that company’s have policies however you would think when something unthinkable happens like a sudden death and that person can no longer come on holidays and you have to cancel you would think you would receive a refund but no. The booking was still 7 days away but that’s not good enough with their 14 day policy

Stayed for 3 nights over Australia Day Weekend. Had a great time. Powered grass site was very large, great amenities and clean. Big camp kitchen. Staff were friendly and amazing helping us with an electric cord that I left at home. Nice walks and view of the water. Cant wait to take my grand kids there as they would love the park and jumping pillow. Cafe with coffee and other essentials. Only 4 km to town. Highly recommend.

This is our second time staying. We absolutely love this campsite. Lots of room on the powered grass sites. The amenity’s always clean and modern. The cafe is a lovely touch, enjoyed my morning coffee there while my son jumped on the bouncy pillow. Staff were friendly and helpful at every opportunity. Highly recommend. Also bonus that it’s so close to the boat ramp and it’s dog friendly!

We stayed in our caravan in the unpowered area. Great location, pick your own area where you want to camp and plenty of space. We thought we would be miles from the toilets but they were actually pretty close.|Nice clean toilets and showers with plenty of pressure.

Enjoyed our stay over Christmas/New Year period. Family of 4 in a 2 bedroom spa cabin. Cabins are clean, but old and in need of some updating. The park is missing a pool or water play area for kids and some shade over the playground.  Unfortunately we were denied fresh towels and advised they were not changed until day 8/9 of our stay (after originally being told it was day 5). Would recommend bringing your own, especially with busy kids, they get dirty quickly! There are laundry facilities to wash your own towels. Check in and out was easy and the text message communication from management was simple and effective.

Grassed powered site was spacious. We had a large storm and flash flooding, thankfully we were on higher ground and didn't get swamped.
The amenities we used were old but functional for the most part.
I thought they may clean them more often in peak time (only once a day, sometimes longer).
Midgies are horrid.
Staff were ok, reception staff nice but ground staff were not rude but not friendly.
Probably wouldn't come back if given another option.
Not bad but not amazing.

The workers were extremely rude and treated my children horribly. They were disrespectful when it came to ordering food. Judging how much they bought. Telling my son that the icecream he was eating was 6 servings and he shouldnt be eating that much if he was going to stay skinny. The location was beautiful however I recommend visiting a different resort at the same location. Also one of the arcade machines took the money from our account but didnt work. We went to the service and they said it happens often but they couldnt do anything and they hadnt put up a sign to say it wasnt working.

Got a spot out the back unpowered $46
Lots of grass. Close to clean showers & toilets. Good 4g. A quiet area. Fires allowed in firepits.

As much as we love Rainbow Beach. We were eaten alive here by midges, mozzies etc. However
friendly staff, clean facilities at the Park.
Been here before I loved it then this time I am staying in a cabin so I not sure what is going to be like 😅
A very comfortable couple of nights. Had a Thai food van on the Friday night which was great!

Amazing stay at the Big 4 Rainbow beach. We camped at their unpowered site. What makes this place totally different is the level of cleanliness of their amenities. Clean nice toilet with hot shower. Heaps of barbie. Spacious aircon kitchen with fridge provided as well. Didn’t have trouble with our neighbors so that’s a bonus. Very happy with our stay🏕️

What a beautiful park so clean
We were camping the camp sites are nice and big plenty of room not everyone is on top of each other
The two lady’s at reception Diann &vicky so so friendly and helpful I’d give this park a 10 out of 10
Me and my family will definitely be back
Regards paul

Absolutely terrible! Overcharged,  then not even water in the fridge...

Then bunk beds Absolutely unsafe (have videos not bolted together and safety loose..

Kids tried to hire pedal carts at 4pm.. was told they shut at 5pm and it's too hard to get the carts back in time for the workers to leave...

Oh did I mention it's my middle lads birthday and this attitude has ruined his birthday...

Great staff and grounds... NOT AT ALL!
Yeah it was good for the price. I'd be spewing if I paid premium.
The midges are terrible
Beautiful place! The service is great
Great place to stay... good value
Amazing stay! Excellent and friendly service. Big problem was we got carried away by sand flies. Its such a problem I don't think we will come back. Bath in Bushman's and you might get bitten less

Great Holiday Park 😀 2024
Absolutely love staying here. !!
Wouldn't stay anywhere else!
ALL Amenities Blocks  , Laundry, & Kitchen Facilities  are kept 100% Clean & Tidy all day every day
Even All the grounds maintenance services and outdoor facilities are kept  spotless at all times.
Staff are always friendly & helpful
Special Mention to Management Peter & Vicki .....  Wow what a Huge Park   -  😁 Congratulations You both do an amazingly fantastic job 👍 Thanks Heaps for allowing me to continue to stay with you guys 🙏
I will not be returning

On our last visit to Carlo Point caravan park a couple of years ago, my little dog was very welcome in reception and the lovely staff were fussing over her. This weekend I was quite abruptly told you can't bring the dog in here even though I was carrying her. I said 'oh ok' and started walking out, the snappy receptionist then told me you can't have dogs in any buildings. There were no obvious signs stating dogs are not allowed and she was also abrupt and snappy with my husband when he was checking in. Luckily we didn't have to go near reception for the rest of the weekend.

We stayed on powered site E1 which was spacious, shaded with big trees, direct access to the inlet for fishing. Just perfect!

They don't squish in campers here, which makes it extremely pleasant to stay.

The kiosk, laundry facilities, playground, jumping pillow and hire bikes were big hits for our family.

Definitely plan for midges & mosquitos if you're visiting in the warmer months. Take insect repellent & calamine lotion or equivalent to ease the itching.

I stayed here five nights over the long weekend in October and had a fantastic experience. The cabin was neat and well-maintained, with a cute balcony. Despite the park being quite busy, the staff at reception were always attentive, friendly, and very efficient, handling everyone's needs with kindness and patience.
The entire park, from the grounds to the facilities, was kept clean throughout my stay. The on-site coffee was a bonus—convenient and tasted good! Whether staying in a cabin, bringing a caravan or motorhome, or simply pitching a tent, this is a great place to enjoy Rainbow Beach.
We camped in the unpowered area, what a beautiful spot! Bring spray to deter the midges

This pace is wonderful, very nice for kids,
My family loved it and we will be back soon
Great park. Nice facilities not bad priced. Very happy to recommend camped in the unpowered area. Then used their caravan storage area for a week

This a great park but if you are after piece and quiet make sure you avoid the school holiday periods.
All the amenities are great but unfortunately we made the mistake of staying during the Queensland school holidays. It seems that camping manners and etiquette is very thin on the ground when the kids are here on mass.
Large park with plenty of space, we could have put 2 vans on our sight E4 3 km out of town

A really great park, great amenities, camp kitchen and location. We camped in the unpowered area and it was lovely and peaceful. Loved being entertained by the plover family and chicks, and the sunset was one of the best we’d seen. However, we were eaten alive by the midges (sandflies) post shower after washing off the deet. Still suffering from the bites all over my body days later. Go prepared to live in deet.
Great park to stay  wonderful friendly staff always pleasant  best amenities  aleaysthanks Dianne  for help
Great Park for kids

Super camping très grand au milieu des arbres et de la nature. Emplacement au calme loin de la route qui permet de se relaxer.
Jeux pour enfants à l'entrée du camping, possibilité excursions et location de matériel. Au top. Rien à redire

The staff here were very friendly and helpful the location is superb off the main road near the Inlet with stunning sunsets. The cabins are spacious. However they’re very frugal with their cutlery crockery  two of us  and there were only two of everything.

Great caravan park, nice big sites, beautifully maintained gardens, toilets.
Friendly staff. Great spot to stay and explore the area.
Wonderful place and great staff

We stayed there for about 10 days. Great location, and plenty of room. We were able to have a fire every afternoon. We decided to stay longer and we had to move to a non powered site. Two other couples joined us with their vans. I made their bookings ahead. On couple arrived and the other couple had some car trouble and missed the first night. The other couple informed the office that they weren’t coming that night on their arrival. Their booking was canceled and we were not able to get a refund. All I asked was that she could defer that booking for the following days but she wouldn’t do that and I had to pay extra for their stay. The other 2 younger staff members were great to get along with. Due to her terrible attitude and the sandflies we will never go back there.
Propreté des sanitaires laissant à désirer (emplacements pour tentes et camping cars).
One of Australia's best places to stay.    Scenery,  space,   water, great facilities abs friendly staff all at a great price

We spent 3 nights here, in our roof tent, without electricity. There is plenty of space to park.

The shower and toilet cabins are great, you feel like at home 😍🫧
It feels good it is always clean !

A thai food truck came on friday night, it was delicious 🤤

The staff is lovely. We will come back with pleasure.

Nice and clean caravan park situated at Carlo point. Lots of room, cabin we stayed in nice and clean. Has jumping pillow, playground, lots of space for kids to ride around. Has very nice camping kitchen and facilities available. Friendly staff, walking distance to the boat ramp and spots for fishing. Known to have sandflies and mozzies but had no problems while there. Has lots grassy areas for campers and lots of cabins of various sizes.

A lovely place, though narrow sites. Views of the beach
The only and big drawback is don't stay here if you need to use the facilities. Radio station on 24/7 in the bathrooms playing very loud 1970s 80s music with ads. Even at 4am it  blasts away your peaceful call of nature.

We really enjoyed our stay at Big 4 Breeze Rainbow beach. Great location, our 2 bedroom spa cabin was clean and perfect for our family of 4. Kids loved the games room, jumping pillow and playground.

The staff were friendly and helpful. We had to reschedule our booking a few times and they were so accommodating. We will definitely be back!

We are a few minutes before 5pm and the staff were not happy,  kept saying we are closing and you should have booked in early.  We didn't know we had too. I told the lady this was our first time to stay in a Big4 camp.
It was very expensive for a power site and we felt not worth being a member of this Big 4 Camping group 😑
My wife fell ill and had to go to the hospital during our stay. The staff were kind, helpful, caring and just brilliant. Wonderful park as well as wonderful staff.

Disappointed and come here alot
For 5 days
I would juyys like to say the the shower head to have a shower was broken and would not stand up by itself,
Yhe front screen door keep falling off the hinge

And the shower door was very hard to open

We come here the  for family holidays  disappointed,
It's like they didn't care to check the manainnace before new guest arrived.

The room also had a wet smell the whole time , that was unpleasant

Disgustingly rude staff, every simple question or request was met with extreme rudeness. No wifi, tiny TV, bare minimum of items such as bin liners provided. Vacation parks are meant to be fun! Children spoken to rudely when asking about hiring bikes, simple requests met with complete arrogance. Would not return.

We stayed at an unpowered site with our dog during Easter 🐣 The girls at the desk were so lovely and accommodating. Firewood and ice prices were reasonable. Kids loved the slushie machine. We got regular updates on the Thai food van which had great food, pizza night and activities during Easter for the kids. Our kids had fun with games and the jumping pillow. We sat by the jetty for sunset drinks, beautiful view. Amenities and bins were always getting cleaned and everything was so organised. So handy with a reasonably priced laundromat whilst staying. Camp kitchen was so clean and easy to wash up. Having our dog as part of the family made our holiday. Kids sat by the fire to eat at night and chat about their day. Great holiday ☺️

We were there with a fishing club. We had a great time, the park is well looked after, toilets and showers are in excellent condition. Staff are super friendly and helpful. Definitely worth going to. We will be back.

We had a caravan site in the Waterview area. Nice and spacious. Close to amenities and camp kitchen.
Staff were excellent, could not do enough for us, communication was great we received text messages of any power outages during our stay as they were having construction works on the property.
VERY lovely park. Facilities were immaculate. Only thing missing is a pool.
Only stayed one night during the week, in my own caravan. The place was so clean and well looked after. Would be awesome for families or groups. So much to do for the kids.
Très belle expérience dans ce camping, avec des facilities vraiment propre et un accueil très sympathique !
Great place but near camp kitchen use your Aeroguard plenty midgies as there is plenty mangroves but don't be put off you will enjoy.

Was charged at peak seasonal rates whilst at the park it was pretty much empty. Sand fly marks all over after spending a night there (not camp grounds fault but hard hit on location). Copious coins required arcadic games that had loud music and lights that played through the night (nobody using them- just a waste of electricity). Camp ground not having a pool like other big 4, but charging same level as the other big 4 (bit odd). - stay was a month ago
Camped here for 2 nights, the shower block and kitchen were new and well appointed, super little herb garden at the kitchen too.  great place for a quiet break
Lovely camping park with excellent facilities and a great location.

Hello Team,

Just a quick note to say thank you soo much for our stay with you last weekend.

We had an amazing time and could not believe the level of service that you offer.

From the guys out on the grounds to the Ladies in the reception, you all are fantastic, Thank you, on the Saturday when I came into the reception and asked if there was a chance that there was a cabin available as my Husband was attacked by mozzies and midges on the Friday night, there was no issue and we had a cabin that morning.

We had a great time with you and we were extremely surprised by the level of service that you provide, we will be back later in the year to stay with you again.

Also (sorry I have forgotten your name) the lady in reception that offered to look after our 2 Dachshund’s on the Saturday so we could go to the lookout, a HUGE THANK YOU to you, that is going above and beyond and it was greatly appreciated.

Once again, thank you soo much for making our stay great.

I was really looking forward to camping here. I had read many good reviews and Rainbow Beach was somewhere I enjoyed visiting when I was a kid. However, I can’t agree with reviews I have read. I initially rang over concern with the weather and was greeted by an extremely rude team member who left a bad taste in my mouth. I still turned up knowing that the weather was going to be ok. I settled into the camp spot but so did some very loud and rude guests who felt the need to have music and talk loudly til early hours of the morning. Now, I messaged the after hours number and was assured that it will be looked into but nothing was done. So, since the team had disregarded their own park rules and the respect of their guests I disregarded the park and left. I won’t be recommending anyone to stay there in future and I will never be returning.

No pool, no beach within 3km, but lots of midgies, mosquitoes, flies and horseflies. Instead of a beach there is a jetty and mangrove swampy area not suitable for swimming. Couple this with no pool and insects and it is unbearable to be here in summer; we left a night early. The grounds are dirt which makes showers muddy, but I know this is an issue at many other parks. It is a 3km walk to the nearest beach and accompanying shops so not convenient at all, especially when wanting to have a few drinks and not being able to drive. On our last night there was a huge domestic dispute in an adjacent site, making us scared and unable to sleep from the endless yelling and threats of violence. Police were called, but it seems the patrons weren’t removed by the park as they stayed and continued to yell throughout the night, despite surrounding campers yelling at them to stop. Overall, would definitely not recommend coming here, maybe winter would be more bearable though.

Amenities at the park were fine, they even have a games section for kids in the laundry facilities! Location was excellent, but mozzies are an issue that the park absolutely cannot do anything about - bring your own repellent and do not rate them 1 star for a darn mosquito issue. We had a Superior 2 bedroom cabin for about $220 a night. It was not superior but it was fine as it met our needs and was pet friendly. I would reduce the price, though. Would stay was not over a public holiday or school holidays. The Wifi is non-existent in the cabin - but don’t ask the staff because they will ring out the tune of “welcome to Rainbow Beach” (absolutely not okay as a response, and we are 24 years into a new century now loaded with tech, so I expect Wifi in the cabin especially when Agoda advertises it as being “in the room”). Bring your own wifi extender to save yourselves a 1.5 hour trip to Hervey Bay and $70 chasing one so you can get a little work done while on holiday (yes, people do use holidays to catch up on a little work!)). There was no dishwasher, and they request you clean your dishes or face a cleaning fee (understandable if there were a tonne of dishes, but a couple would be absurd). My only concern here is that you are trusting former guests to clean the dishes etc properly - which they did not, unfortunately. The TV in the main bedroom was a 14 inch jammed on top of a small cabinet, which only gained comedic value as even Michael Scott in The Office wall mounted his 14 inch TV 😂 But this was fine as we didn’t actually expect any TVs and were out from 6AM-4PM each day. The shower had rust coloured staining. Do not expect cabin servicing, even for a 3 night stay at least to my knowledge.

I would have rated this 5 star if the value met my expectations. Like $150 a night, and I would expect this.

All in all, the vacation was great.
Carlo Point 👉

Declared tropiclal cyclone off coast of QLD with people being warned not to travel and the caravan park will not allow moving the booking even though there are flash flooding alerts for time we booked the site. Money grabbing, greedy operators with no regards for their customers or statewide messaging put out by the government. Can't find anything in small print about it being during peak period exclusion and they do not display dates to tell you when peak is so entirely up to the parks interpretation. I have been here many times as have friends - I will be warning them not to stay here and use other facilities in Rainbow Beach.
Clean cabins but the absence of a pool or decent games room means that fun for the kids will need to be found elsewhere

Absolutely loved our stay. Super friendly and family oriented. Somewhere we felt safe with the kids. Highly recommended. Most definitely will be coming back!!!
The facilities were clean and well maintained aswell. Could only give 5 stars would give more!!
No swimming pool onsite.  Park not maintained well ie lots pot holes around park, just needs filling with gravel.  Large sites for caravans. Toilet shower blocks full of mould .  We won’t be back.

Have stayed here 2 times now, first in a cabin second on a powered site with our camper. Park atmosphere is great play grounds for the kids. Washing in laundry is $5 to wash and $1 for every 10min to dry. Make sure you have $1 coins for the dryers. The camp kitchen is outstanding with 3 sinks, 3 microwave, 3 big fridges and 3 toasters. It also has hot water boilers with instant boiling water.
On the weekend Friday Saturday night saw a Thai Street Food truck operate from 1pm to 8pm. Great food and very reasonable prices.

Had a great stay in studio cabin, clean as a whistle, great air conditioning and everything you need. Fantastic food vans visit regularly. Well run and felt very safe as a person travelling alone. Thank you

Bel emplacement
Avec un dauphin à demeure dans la baie
Par contre les douches pas suffisantes  avec le sable compliqué et de nombreux moustiques

We stayed in a 2 room cabin. It was lovely and clean,  however my husband and I were bitten very badly by bed bugs. I'm still so itchy, it's driving me crazy.
The kids in the other room were fine. No it was not sand flys or midges,  you can see them.  The timing and pattern of the bites indicates bed bugs 100%. I'm a registered nurse with advanced training,  I know what I'm looking at.
I sent 2 emails and made a phone call.  Not reply email came and the reception lady was quite rude. She basically told me it could not possibly be bed bugs because they change the sheet and bom regularly.  That's great , I'm sure you do, alas a fumigation is due again. Bed bugs do not live in sheet, the bites often appear 1-2 days after, can leave you itchy for up to 2wks, bites are ften in a line as the bugs move.
I paid over $700 for the privilege to be eaten my your parasitic infestation.  No one has offered me a refund, compensation or even an apology.
I will not be recommending Brezze big4 Park to anyone.

Nice cabins, clean with nice equipment.

Very kid friendly environment.

Flight was delayed and had contacted the campsite to advise that we would be arriving late; was met by an unfriendly staff member who was rude on the phone. When arriving we had realised we had booked for the wrong date, rather than being accommodating the female staff member huffed and puffed and hung up the phone. Leaving us with no where to camp and paying for a site we never saw.
Great place to bring the family.

This camping site is very big, with both cabins and camp-out areas. Pricing was okay with many activities such as a kayaking, jumpy pillow, and many more near this location. Watch out for the sandflies however with spray. Dog friendly. The floor gets muddy when rain comes but has potential in good weather. Showers had dirty floors but will get cleaned everyday. There was a kitchen for you to cook and store food in the fridge.
Amazing location, great for the family.
Nice large caravan sides. Very clean grounds and amenities. The only thing missing is a swimming pool.
Great place to stay close to the water and good fishing
Beautiful setting. Clean cabins & amenities. Staff go above & beyond to help their guests with their needs.
Awesome caravan Park

Awesome time 🙌 We stayed in a 3 bed cabin mid week getaway, the cabin was great very spacious and clean , All staff we very helpful and friendly , couldn’t fault it ! Highly recommend. We can’t wait to go again. The park and ammenities were excellent.

A pool would be good 😊
Not as clean as expected. Unpowered camping was difficult to find a spot. Very dark navigating through at night.
Stayed on the way to Fraser and was clean and had excellent facilities
Wish we could have stayed longer
What a beautiful spot Close to the water amities very good
Roomy

Overall a lovely stay, the cabin was modern and very clean. Unfortunately during our stay there was a burst water pipe, however water bottles were kindly handed out. This just made showering and washing up… Not possible!

The reception staff were friendly and helpful, in particular Linda. As backpackers without a car we found the location quite difficult! But Linda kindly organised us a taxi and even offered to drive us herself to ensure we didn’t miss our Greyhound bus.

I would stay here again and would recommend, however only for people that have access to a car.

So much fun at big 4! We stayed on a two bedroom cabin with our dog. Only thing is the bunk beds creak so much but everything is clean and lovely otherwise. Kitchen has the basics. Kids loved the jumping pillow and nice to have a park there. If there was a pool would be epic ! There’s access to the water though, although sandflies are around so make sure you have some repellent !

Myself and 2 other families with young kids had the best week at this campsite! So good we wanted to stay longer!
All the facilities were amazing (between cabins and campsites), staff always helpful and kids had a blast!
Mozzies are annoying but that can’t be helped!
Fantastic location and toilets and showers always being cleaned.
I even left hair straighteners behind that were found and posted home quickly to me.
We’ll be back for sure!

Stayed here on an unpowered site for 3 nights on our way off of Fraser. Great location. Nice flat, shaded sites. Lovely clean bathroom amenities which were ensuite set up. Great camp kitchen area. Close to the water edge. Few midgies at night so be prepared to cover up. Facilities: laundry, bouncy pillow, playground, games room, cart hire.
Stayed in a cabin with our dog. Super location, very clean and comfortable cabin, and the team at Big 4 Rainbow Beach are really helpful and friendly.

Too expensive for what is offered.
We had the "bad luck" of not having water for a full day.
Good location.

Sorry to say very disappointed in our stay office staff so rude, one in particular. Nothing to do on site most of kids games not working.
We came for a family holiday the cabin my parents stayed in was just not good Air con not working, bathroom very unsafe for there age not happy ruined our stay will not go back

We had a powered sites which was very nice and near the facilities as requested.After the school holidays it was very quiet which suited us.I would highly recommend this park and we will be returning for sure.
We camper here everything about the place is lovely apart from mossies or sand flies. They ate us alive

Tough review this one as arrived late at night and struggled to find a decent pitch but that just goes to show how popular this site is.

Decent size camp kitchen with the usual amenities and plenty of place to sit.

The wash house was ‘ok’ comparing it to previous sites this was probably the worst but we arrived late and left early so probably saw it at its worst.

Staff really friendly and went out of their way to help. Nice a quiet through the night all in all an ok stop over before our Fraser Island tour.

Would i stay again. Probably as there is very little nearby.

One of the best caravan parks I’ve stayed in. Would have stayed longer but was booked out!  Finally a caravan park that understands you don’t want to be camped on top of each other!  So much room/ shade to be found, can hire fire pits/ buy wood etc. staff were amazing especially Rachel who was so kind to everyone even during hectic busy times. Highly recommend to others
Great place to stay. Lovely surroundings. Will come back sometime 😊

Exceeded expectations. It was very busy being school holidays but the amenities were clean and modern. Recommend not getting a slab site as the sites under the trees were much bigger and nicer. Stones throw from the water with lovely sunsets.

Very modern holiday park, we used unpowered sites which were great. Lots of room and shade. Amenities were fantastic, very clean and easily accessible.
Midgies and mozzies are quite bad here so pack all your bug repellent.

Made to feel at home here!
Beautiful camping sites, modern and well kept amenities - wonderful team working here looking after the site and everyone.
Absolutely the best spot to be staying in town & faces west for the most gorgeous  sunsets

Found this park very quiet at night out of school holidays..big comfortable site, great view of the water thru the trees..amenities could do with upgrade and it surprises me there is no pool considering the nightly rate,as the beach needs to be driven to, however, would gladly stay again
Had the most awesome time we extended our trip for an extra night.

Way too rigid on check-in time. We were not aware that caravan check-in isn't until midday (which we very rarely arrive before anyway at any park). Saw the sign as we pulled into their driveway which is obviously a bit late to do anything about. Sent on our way again at 11:20. Unpowered site which is unallocated - can't see the issue letting people in a little early. Came back at about 12:30 and surprise surprise people are ramped up across the road and everyone in the office is under the pump....
We got smashed by midgees too, not blaming BIG4 for that, it's just a downside of staying there, likely seasonal 🤷🏼‍♂️

Es un camping grande, dividido en dos zonas, una mas "urbana" y otra mas salvaje.  Los atradeceres son increibles. A unos 200m tienes un pequeño puerto desde donde salen algunos barcos, tambien hay un sitio para hacer buceo, ademas de un restaurante. El camping dispone de diferentes baños y cocinas totalmente equipadas. La lavanderia esta situada en la zona mas urbanizada, donde estan los bungalows.

Got a camp site right on the sea front. 48 bucks. Amazing spot and not only that but when I went for a piss in the woods I came across a freekin surf club. 10m from the site hidden.... Food, wine, cocktails and sunset. Two weeks traveling the Queensland cost and fond a gem. Thanks guy's. Rainbow bay rocks.

We had a nice spot and the facilities are clean and new, would’ve been a 5 star stay except for the grumpy groundsman who decided to circle our camp with his mower for 20 minutes. In the process he drove over the remains of peoples campfires and over sand covering our car and tent in ash, sand and grass. Had to go to a car wash afterwards and scrub it all off.
Best Holiday Park I’ve visited

Loved the place. Sites with good views to the Great Sandy Strait. Very modern, clean and comfortable camp kitchen and toilets. Each toilet has its own washbasin and shower. Plenty of space. Great 4G reception with Optus and Telstra (we work online). Near Rainbow Beach to cross to K'gari. The reception ladies were lovely.
An enormous caravan park about 3kms from Rainbow Beach. Friendly staff and service. Our caravan spot was good and close to everything. Would definitely return if we were coming to this area.
Had a great time away, rooms very clean and spacious. Staff are very busy but always pleasant. Midges galore. Cabins where perfect, comfy beds plenty of room. This park is massive.

Deze camping had een groot veld waar je een eige plaats kon kiezen om te gaan staan met je camper. De zonsondergang hier is magistraal. Rainbow beach zelf viel een beetje tegen. Strand was smal en had veel zwart zand. Oud en nieuw sanitair. Ik zou het nieuwe kiezen 😉||We zijn bij de camping gaan uiteten. Verrassend lekker met een mooie zonsondergang.
We are regularly here it is our family holiday spot, the office staff are amazing and friendly but most of all they remember you and your families it's definitely our home away from home

We've been coming here for years and every year the park has upgraded one part or another. Facilities are excellent, the place is well run with well trained and responsive staff. The relaxed vibe always makes our stay memorable and enjoyable.
Great park
Fantastic facilities and location, will be back for sure

A very spacious caravan park set in a very bushy landscape. Combination of first class cabins and caravan sites.

Generally you will be able to experience a very level, and easily accessible van of camp site. Well positioned to the Carlo Point boat ramp and inlet.

Very relaxing location. Amenities are high quality, both clean and well care for.

Amazing campground with loads of sites and cabins. Terrific location directly at the water and in close proximity to Tin Can Bay and Inskip Point. Lovely staff for all our questions and centrally in the park, wifi worked well. Camp kitchen is decent too and a rented kayak was affordable and well-suited for two amateurs!

On the bay, fishing trips leave from here.
Large non crowded caravan sites.
Everything is very new and clean.
You can have fires in fire pits.
1 of my favourite stays.
Unpowered waterfront camping, easy, comfortable and the amenities are A1.  10/10.
Awesome place to stay

We were so disappointed with our powered site! Dirty, black sand covered the ground surrounding our motor home. The sand stuck to our thongs and was so deep in places we couldn’t ride our electric bikes.  Our site was a long way from the amenities. Not all showers were open and the floor of my shower was flooded. Instead of staying 3 nights we left after one night. The park is isolated with no beach accessibility.
Could not of been better

We stayed at lovely Rainbow Beach BIG4 for 2 nights in the dog friendly powered area and loved it! The staff were friendly, the amenities were clean and modern and there was a delicious pizza van for dinner on the Saturday. It was close to the town centre and beaches too. A great stay!
My room was smelly. I asked for an alternative option, I was offered something with half the size. I ended up sleeping in the car for the whole 3 nights as I couldn’t tolerate the smell.
Best caravan park in Rainbow Beach.

Stayed here for 3 nights for trip to Frazer Island. Large, easy accessible sites. No TV reception, they provide cords but still not much  good.  Nice  clean amenities. Handy to boat ramp, restaurant and sunset viewing.
Great park.
Large clean and well organised park. Staff were friendly and helpful.  Amenities could of been cleaned more frequently on weekend.

Booked for 2 nights and stayed 12 we loved it so much. Staff were just lovely, park was beautifully maintained and the sunset every afternoon was breathtaking. We had e-bikes and found it easy enough to ride everyday into town.

Great place to stay to explore rainbow beach and Fraser Island. We camped (unpowered) here both two nights before and two nights after visiting Fraser Island and it was absolutely perfect for our needs and value for money. The amenities were spotless and modern, the camp kitchen is brilliant and the park itself is really well presented with plenty of space and beautiful views of the sunset as well as great stars at night!
Great caravan park with loads of space and huge space for your van. Very busy with kids on weekends as they have playgrounds etc for kids. Great spot down near the boat ramp.

Hi! All
Family and friends stayed here and they were very pleased.
Best Wishes 🌠
Tom 🙂🚂🚂
Loved this place. Totally awesome
5 stars only for the music in the toilets!! So cool!! Very clean toilets all the time. I just suggest to fix the kitchen area close to the beach :-)

This is the second time we have stayed here. On both occasions, we have a powered caravan site behind the cabins. The sites are a good size and easy to reverse into. The staff are friendly and the park is well maintained.  It's a short walk to the boat ramp and a restaurant. It's only a short trip into town for coffee, lunch or dinner.
Carlo point is an ideal camping area, maybe one of the best you will stay at. The sunsets are magnificent.  Staff are friendly, amenities are always clean and tidy.
